title: Auto Liability Limits
content: 25/50/25 means $25k per person bodily injury, $50k per accident bodily injury, $25k property damage. This is the minimum coverage required in most states.

title: Claim Deductibles
content: Deductible is the amount paid by customer before insurance covers the rest. Common amounts: $250, $500, $1000. Higher deductibles typically mean lower premiums.

title: Homeowners Coverage Types
content: HO-3 is most common - covers dwelling and personal property. HO-5 offers more comprehensive coverage. HO-6 is for condos.

title: Umbrella Insurance
content: Provides extra liability coverage beyond auto and home policies. Typically $1M-$5M. Protects assets from lawsuits.

title: Filing a Claim Process
content: Report claim ASAP. Document damage with photos. Get repair estimates. Adjuster will inspect. Settlement issued after review.

title: Proof of Insurance
content: Certificate of Insurance (COI) shows coverage details. Required for contractors, landlords, events. Valid 30-90 days typically.

title: Commercial Auto Policy
content: Covers business vehicles and drivers. Different from personal auto. Includes hired/non-owned auto coverage options.

title: Workers Compensation
content: Required in most states for businesses with employees. Covers medical costs and lost wages for work injuries.

title: General Liability Policy
content: CGL covers bodily injury and property damage claims. Essential for businesses. Common limits: $1M per occurrence, $2M aggregate.

title: Premium Calculation Factors
content: Auto: driving record, age, location, vehicle. Home: location, construction, claims history, credit score.
